speaker: Davicho Barona
text: Give it as much context as you possibly can.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: And what I mean by that, too, is not just context of what you are… trying to… create, But…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: of why. Like, what your reasoning is behind doing certain things. Like, for example, a lot of times, I will tell the agent to create something with multiple image models.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: But I won't just say, make it with these models. I'll say, make it with these models, so that I can…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: compare outputs. And by adding that final little sign-off of, so that I can compare outputs.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: I'm telling the agent why I'm doing this. Its responses are going to be different than if you didn't say that. It will probably organize them differently, it'll title them differently, and then it'll follow up differently. It'll say, how's this working? Which one works best? Do you want to, like.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: do something according to this, because you're… you know what I mean? So, giving it real context of not only what you're trying to do, but why.
